meaning: n.芭贝特(Elizabeth的法语形式昵称,亦作Barbette)(f.)
examples: No one else was in the room but the wise man's servant, whose name was Babette.
Babette, a young journalist, has investigated the Mafia only to see her lover, a Roman lawyer, hideously murdered.
Babette Clarke was 11 when she headed to the shelter in 1943.
His survivors include his wife, Babette, and five children.
